rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds from the requirement.
Do not generate implementation-level guarantees; use assign only for output bindings in implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must reference only visible, typed features or declared auxiliaries from the AADL model.
Use boolean helper equations for range checks, ensuring type alignment with source data (real/int/bool).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use the format: annex agree {** ... **};
Range constraints should be expressed as eq helper: bool = x >= min and x <= max;
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid converting Boolean or opaque ports into numeric range comparisons without explicit scalar representation.
